steps.title: Spot the moment of heightened awareness
steps.content: The narrator's turning point is the statement: “Never, however, was I so aware of entering a new stage as I was that afternoon in October.” This is the crux—she has faced many unexpected life moments, but this particular instance is unique because she consciously recognizes it as a transition between life stages.

steps.title: Match the awareness to the answer
steps.content:
C states exactly what the passage shows: the narrator “recognizes that a particular moment marks a major shift in her life.” The phrase entering a new stage aligns with major shift, and that afternoon in October is the particular moment.

The other choices either invent details not in the text (A's business, D's gratitude) or contradict it (B claims she always planned ahead when she explicitly says she was often unprepared).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
